items.headline: Crypto Clarity Act in spotlight for bad-actor provisions as Senate process grinds forward
items.subheadline: The industry has been trying to make a case this week the Clarity Act provides law enforcement strong tools to combat illicit finance involving cryptocurrency.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: U.S. House tax committee weighs crypto bills, including relief for small transactions
items.subheadline: Seven draft bills are being circulated by the House Ways and Means Committee ahead of a hearing next week, including proposals to ease small-gain, mining and staking burdens.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: A quick review of the Ways and Means tax bills: State of Crypto
items.subheadline: The House Ways and Means Committee is gearing up for its big tax push.
items.author: Nikhilesh De

items.headline: What American crypto asset perpetuals mean for the future of crypto
items.subheadline: For years, one of the most significant crypto asset markets has existed entirely outside the United States. Today, that changes, explains CFTC Chairman Selig.
items.author: Michael S. Selig

items.headline: How to better understand bitcoin’s perpetual identity crisis
items.subheadline: Here, Bailey unpacks the uncertainty surrounding the original cryptocurrency’s nature, which creates inconsistent market behavior.
items.author: Steve Bailey

items.headline: Why tokenization is an ETF-style market structure revolution
items.subheadline: The current tokenization dialogue and pattern resemble ETFs’ early days, which ultimately transformed into a $10+ trillion market, Lie argues.
items.author: Michael Lie

items.headline: Meta is paying creators in Stablecoins. Spending them is someone else's problem
items.subheadline: Meta’s decision to pay creators in USDC validates stablecoins as a mainstream disbursement tool, Joslyn suggests, but it also exposes the industry’s unresolved problem: moving seamlessly from digital dollars to usable local currency.
items.author: Tim Joslyn

items.headline: Gold slips below 200-day moving average offering glimmer of hope for bitcoin bulls
items.subheadline: Gold falls into bear market territory, while a stronger U.S. dollar and rising rate expectations pressure risk assets.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Bitcoin holds steady after Sunday's rally, though full-fledged reversal may take longer
items.subheadline: Bitcoin is holding above $63,000 after rallying on Sunday. The price is now hovering near a key 200-week moving average that often marks major cycle turning points.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: UK House of Lords committee calls on Bank of England to reconsider proposed stablecoin restrictions
items.subheadline: The Bank of England proposed limits of 20,000 pounds per coin for individuals and 10 million pounds for businesses.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: How DeFi is changing the financial landscape for Latin Americans
items.subheadline: DeFi is quietly shifting from niche crypto experiment to a legitimate financial tool across the region, explains Serrano.
items.author: Sebastián Serrano

items.headline: Bitcoin’s hash rate dwarfs top 100 supercomputers by 600k times, says Bittensor co-founder
items.subheadline: Ala Shaabana explains how using Bitcoin network’s coordinate-and-reward playbook can bust open corporate monopolies on AI.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: CME is letting traders bet on bitcoin volatility, not price, and two firms have already placed bets
items.subheadline: Monarq and DV Chain kick off trading in CME's bitcoin volatility index futures.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Moomoo adds Kalshi prediction markets, giving users access to event contracts
items.subheadline: Retail trading platform moomoo has partnered with Kalshi to offer CFTC-regulated event contracts tied to economic data, elections and major sporting events directly within its app.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Tokenization specialist Securitize clears key hurdle to go public on NYSE
items.subheadline: The tokenization specialist behind BlackRock's BUIDL fund could begin trading on the NYSE as SEC approves merger registration.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Why quantitative traders are using complex math models to hijack your weekend sports bets
items.subheadline: While rising volume on Polymarket and Kalshi is attracting quantitative firms to prediction markets, they aren't focusing on event outcomes; rather, they're exploiting market inefficiencies for profit.
items.author: Oliver Knight

items.headline: Billion-dollar crypto investor doubles down on bitcoin, questions Ethereum's upside
items.subheadline: Sourcing initial capital from his mother to build an inves, DFG CEO James Wo says market metrics do not support Tom Lee’s $250,000 ether prediction.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: The battle for digital dollars is moving onchain
items.subheadline: America’s biggest banks are launching tokenized deposits to compete with stablecoins, opening a new front in the race to become the dominant form of cash on blockchain networks.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Abra CEO Bill Barhydt sees tokenization overtaking bitcoin price as crypto’s main story
items.subheadline: As Abra prepares for a Nasdaq debut, CEO Bill Barhydt is betting tokenized yield products and onchain lending will drive the next phase of crypto wealth management.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Strategy (MSTR) news: Michael Saylor revives bitcoin-buy speculation as scrutiny grows
items.subheadline: The firm's executive chairman posted a familiar chart with Strategy's previous BTC purchases writing "a good time to add more dots."
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: U.S. PCI report, ECB interest-rate decision: Crypto Week Ahead: Crypto Week Ahead
items.subheadline: Your look at what's coming in the week starting June 8.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Trezor says users' crypto is safe after Ledger researchers uncover chip flaw
items.subheadline: The flaw affects only one of the wallet's multiple security layers and would require physical access, specialized equipment and advanced expertise to exploit.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Apparent Zcash outage was a block explorer problem, infrastructure provider says
items.subheadline: One expert said the issue was mainly with block explorers tracking the onchain activity.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) news: Not all layer 2s are dying, but many no longer have a reason to exist
items.subheadline: In this week's edition of The Protocol Newsletter, we're looking at the state of the Ethereum layer-2 ecosystem.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: BTC price updates: Bitcoin could fall to $60,000, Zcash plunges 37%
items.subheadline: AI bets are recording rapid profit-taking as Broadcom as outlook for AI-chip sales fell short of high expectations, driving down crypto markets.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Live updates: bitcoin tumbles to $60,000 as blowout jobs data, Zcash bug keeps pressure on crypto
items.subheadline: Zcash plummetted 40% as Shielded Labs revealed a major bug that went undetected for four years
items.author: Stephen Alpher

items.headline: Security experts warn advanced AI is about to spark a hacking crisis for both crypto and banks
items.subheadline: After an AI model helped uncover a four-year-old flaw in Zcash, security researchers warn that similar bugs may be hiding across crypto and traditional financial systems.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Researcher who found Zcash's bug with AI adds Monero to his audit queue
items.subheadline: Taylor Hornby, who uncovered the Orchard flaw that sent Zcash down 38%, says other privacy coins are on his list too.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Ethereum (ETH) News: Foundation cuts and departures aren't a crisis, Joe Lubin says
items.subheadline: The Consensys CEO told CoinDesk that the Ethereum Foundation's role should be narrower and more focused on stewarding the network's core technology and values.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: Bitcoin spikes, then dumps, from $63,700 as analysts assess Strategy's next BTC moves
items.subheadline: BTC, ETH, XRP and others pulled back from their overnight highs as Iran-Israel tensions and oil rally triggered risk aversion in Asian stocks.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: It’s time for clarity for America’s digital asset markets
items.subheadline: Recent polls have shown that registered voters want America to set the rules for global finance, reinforcing why the Senate must act now, explains Smith.
items.author: Kristin Smith

items.headline: Iran war shows markets no longer sleep
items.subheadline: The latest conflict involving Iran has produced an unexpected proving ground for financial infrastructure, and an unlikely winner has emerged, argues Huang.
items.author: Annabelle Huang

items.headline: The U.S. can’t lose the bitcoin race to China
items.subheadline: The next global power competition is not being fought over missiles alone. It’s being fought over money, and right now, China is moving aggressively to shape the future of it, argues Gooden.
items.author: Congressman Lance Gooden

items.headline: The agentic CFO in your pocket
items.subheadline: Chalom explains that retail investors have never had the opportunity to access and manage their own digital treasury desk. Until now.
items.author: Joseph Chalom

items.headline: The Clarity Act won’t lead to adoption without crypto tax reform
items.subheadline: The legislation is being framed as a turning point for U.S. crypto policy, but there’s a major piece of the puzzle being overlooked, argues Singh.
items.author: Robin Singh

items.headline: A bipartisan bridge to the future: Why the Senate must finish the job on digital Assets
items.subheadline: The Clarity Act’s recent markup proved that the momentum for regulation is there. It is imperative that Congress move the bill forward to establish rules this generation needs and a framework the next will inherit, urges Kim.
items.author: Ji Hun Kim

items.headline: Disciplined AI agents are the disruptor needed to break the exchange churn model
items.subheadline: Programmable incentives that allow independent trading agents to earn only when portfolios rise will create a fairer market for retail customers, explains Naja.
items.author: Saad Naja

items.headline: Banks' survey says people don't want to rock the boat if stablecoin yield risks lending
items.subheadline: The American Bankers Association, which lobbies against the crypto sector over the Clarity Act's stablecoin section, unveiled its new polling.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Premier League soccer clubs warned about unauthorized crypto firms' sponsorship
items.subheadline: The FCA said unauthorized firms could be breaching rules on financial promotions through high-profile sponsorships deals.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: Crypto PACs go undefeated in June primaries as Fairshake scores bipartisan winning streak
items.subheadline: Winners included Democrats Zoe Lofgren, Ted Lieu, Dave Min and Rob Menendez, as well as Republican Sen. Mike Rounds, with Democrats accounting for 10 of the 11 victories.
items.author: Sam Reynolds

items.headline: New DeFi entrant widens field of crypto political campaign funds as elections loom
items.subheadline: The Defend Developers PAC is aiming to contribute to lawmakers who seek to shield crypto developers from legal vulnerabilities.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: JPMorgan sees shrinking window for U.S. crypto market structure overhaul
items.subheadline: The Clarity Act still faces several legislative hurdles, and disputes over stablecoin yield have emerged as a key sticking point, the bank said.
items.author: Will Canny

items.headline: Russia sanctions British teenager for alleging A7A5 use in funding Ukraine war
items.subheadline: The A7A5 stablecoin was designed to bypass sanctions imposed on Russia following its invasion of Ukraine in 2022.
items.author: Jamie Crawley

items.headline: OCC chief says Democrats applying sole political pressure in World Liberty charter choice
items.subheadline: The regulator rejected claims he's doing President Trump's bidding during a congressional hearing that also addressed the GENIUS Act stablecoin push.
items.author: Jesse Hamilton

items.headline: Crypto's recovery remains unsecure as SpaceX, Anthropic IPOs loom. Stronger ETF inflows would help: Crypto Daily
items.subheadline: Your day-ahead look for June 8, 2026
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: Bybit challenges Wall Street with a massive push into tokenized U.S. stock IPOs
items.subheadline: Retail investors can now buy shares at official underwritten prices through the crypto exchange, bypassing Wall Street’s exclusive pre-IPO clubs.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Why a hidden math metric shows bitcoin may be getting too cheap for investors to ignore
items.subheadline: The metric shows bitcoin's market price is getting close to its realized fair value after the recent sell-off.
items.author: Omkar Godbole

items.headline: MSTR buys 1,550 BTC, boosts cash reserves to $1 billion
items.subheadline: Strategy adds bitcoin and cash after raising $181 million through stock sales.
items.author: James Van Straten

items.headline: Ethereum news: Tom Lee's Bitmine (BMNR) bought the dip, acquiring 126,971 ETH as prices tanked
items.subheadline: The company bought 126,971 ETH last week, worth roughly $214 million at current prices, despite chairman Tom Lee's earlier calls to slow purchases.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: MetaMask launches AI agent wallet with built-in security for every crypto trade
items.subheadline: The launch comes as AI agents increasingly emerge as participants in crypto markets, executing trades and managing capital on behalf of users.
items.author: Margaux Nijkerk

items.headline: NEAR gains 12.3% as almost all CoinDesk 20 assets trade higher
items.subheadline: Bittensor (TAO), up 12% from Friday, joined NEAR Protocol (NEAR) as a top performer.
items.author: CoinDesk Indices

items.headline: Bitcoin price analysis: Here is the real reason why bitcoin is crashing, according to an analyst
items.subheadline: The main driver behind bitcoin's weakness was ETF selling after red-hot April U.S. inflation data, 10x's Markus Thielen argued. The bounce may hinge on Wednesday's CPI data, he said.
items.author: Krisztian Sandor

items.headline: Inside the chaotic $300 million emergency bailout that saved a top crypto platform from total collapse
items.subheadline: A close look at the April bridge exploit shows how external infrastructure failures can easily breach the industry's biggest lending reserves.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: The startup killer: Ledger CTO says the EU's crushing compliance costs are choking Web3 innovation
items.subheadline: Industry insiders warn that MiCA's steep financial barriers are choking early-stage innovation.
items.author: Olivier Acuna

items.headline: Sam Bankman-Fried officially asks Donald Trump for a presidential pardon
items.subheadline: The fallen crypto mogul serving a 25-year sentence filed a clemency petition, betting on Donald Trump's history of crypto pardons even though the president had told him not to count on one.
items.author: Helene Braun

items.headline: Live BTC price: Bitcoin drops under $63,000 as Strategy adds $100 million BTC
items.subheadline: Meanwhile, Strive bought 32 bitcoin, the same number Strategy dumped last week.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Citrini, the research firm that caused AI stocks meltdown lays out Hyperliquid as new 'compelling' idea
items.subheadline: The research firm says that, unlike most crypto, Hyperliquid actually generates cash flow and has a token buyback mechanism.
items.author: Aoyon Ashraf

items.headline: A forehead tattoo typo became a $600,000 crypto token, revealing the dark side of memecoin craze
items.subheadline: The stunt turned a typo into a tradable meme, while critics said the episode shows how quickly crypto incentives can get ugly when real people become the content.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Humanity Protocol token crashes more than 80% after a $32 million private-key hack
items.subheadline: The decentralized identity project said attackers compromised the keys of a foundation member and are dumping the stolen H tokens for ether.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Bitcoin can drop to $30,000 but Strategy won't sell, BTC miner says
items.subheadline: Jiang Zhuoer of BTC.TOP called the week's selloff speculation overblown, arguing Strategy's small debt and the design of its preferred shares let it keep buying.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Live Bitcoin price: BTC above $63,000 as some peg SpaceX's IPO as 'next catalyst'
items.subheadline: Stocks recovered through SpaceX's oversubscribed IPO, an Nvidia and SK Hynix chip pact, and Apple's AI reboot, while crypto trailed.
items.author: Shaurya Malwa

items.headline: Saylor blamed AI for bitcoin crash. Arca has one word for that: Nonsense
items.subheadline: Arca is blaming Strategy's sale of 32 BTC for last week's BTC crash, not AI capital rotation, as Strategy's Saylor claimed.
items.author: Omkar Godbole
